authoritative_sources.title: Businessperson's Guide to Federal Warranty Law
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is authoritative for the Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act baseline, including written-warranty structure, dispute framing, and the reminder that state-law remedies can vary materially.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: warranty scope grounding and customer-facing coverage boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 701 - Disclosure of Written Consumer Product Warranty Terms and Conditions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR is the current public codification of FTC warranty rules. Part 701 is authoritative for covered products or parts, remedy statements, warranty term start, exclusions, and consumer obligations that must be visible in the warranty record set.

authoritative_sources.title: Motor Vehicle Safety Defects and Recalls: What Every Vehicle Owner Should Know
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA is the primary U.S. motor-vehicle safety regulator. This official booklet is authoritative for recall initiation, no-charge remedies, dealer repair expectations, reimbursement limits, and recall communication boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recall, stop-sale, and no-charge remedy handling

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 573 - Defect and Noncompliance Responsibility and Reports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 49 CFR Part 573 is authoritative for safety-related defect and noncompliance reporting, dealer sale prohibitions on new defective vehicles and equipment, owner and dealer notifications, and recall-governance obligations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: defect reporting, hold, and sale-prohibition boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: CQI-14-4 | Automotive Warranty Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG is a recognized automotive industry body. CQI-14 is an authoritative automotive warranty-management reference for robust warranty programs, no-trouble-found case handling, and proactive warranty improvement across OEM, dealer, and supplier ecosystems.

authoritative_sources.title: AIAG CQI-14 Warranty Key Terms
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG's public warranty-key-terms reference is authoritative for common automotive warranty terminology such as labor op codes, VIN, no-trouble-found, parts return center, product service manual, TSB, and warranty verbatims.

authoritative_sources.title: CQI-28 | AIAG Traceability Guideline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIAG's traceability guideline is an authoritative automotive reference for product visibility, recall-scope reduction, traceability self-assessment, and traceability fire-drill discipline.

authoritative_sources.title: MMOG/LE (Global Materials Management Operations Guideline)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: MMOG/LE is a recognized global automotive materials-management standard used by OEMs and suppliers to assess supply-chain process capability across development, production, and aftermarket or service phases.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: inventory, backorder, and logistics dependency handling

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Automotive Quality Management System Requirements for Sub-Tier Suppliers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The IATF MAQMSR document is an authoritative public quality reference for supplier QMS development, including control plans, identification and traceability, nonconforming product, customer notification, customer waiver boundaries, part approval, and change control.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized global standards body for interoperable identification and traceability. Its global traceability standard is authoritative for linking identifiers, batches or lots, critical tracking events, key data elements, and targeted recall queries across supply chains.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cross-system lot, serial, and recall-scope linkage

authoritative_sources.title: Customer Specific Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The IATF customer-specific-requirements index is authoritative for showing that OEM-specific quality and PPAP requirements vary by manufacturer and effective date, which constrains when dealer support may rely on public baseline sources alone.

authoritative_sources.title: 2025 Toyota Crown Signia Warranty and Maintenance Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Toyota's public warranty and maintenance guide is authoritative for its own warranty-service workflow and provides concrete automotive evidence expectations around maintenance records, service-provider choice, repair delays, and authorized dealership service handling.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and minimum-facts grounding
workflow_stages.description: Capture tenant, OEM or brand, jurisdiction, VIN, repair-order, warranty or campaign program, part scope, and requested outcome before attempting resolution.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Coverage, claim, and service-record qualification
workflow_stages.description: Verify in-service date, odometer, policy or campaign eligibility, repair-order status, labor-op logic, technician evidence, and customer-facing warranty posture before moving the request forward.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Traceability, inventory, and parts-dependency review
workflow_stages.description: Review causal-part scans, serial or lot genealogy, inventory state, parts restriction, shipment status, and warehouse or supplier records to determine whether the record set supports bounded handling.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Quality hold, recall, and release-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve quality hold, stop-sale, campaign, containment, and nonconformance boundaries whenever safety, release, or suspect-product conditions materially affect the service answer.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Resolution and stakeholder communication packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-backed dealer, customer, or internal stakeholder summaries that explain what is known, what action was taken, what remains blocked, and the next owner without overstating authority.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Repeat-failure, supplier-boundary, and policy-conflict escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate repeat failures, no-trouble-found recurrences, unsupported exceptions, supplier-substitution pressure, and OEM or tenant policy conflicts with the required evidence package and next owner.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: VIN, warranty start date, in-service date, odometer, campaign screen, and ownership or fleet records
repair orders, technician punch data, DTCs, labor op codes, authorization numbers, policy notes, customer verbatims, and service communications
warranty claim records, claim coding, prior approval notes, part-return requests, claim-audit artifacts, and denial rationale
causal-part serial or lot records, parts scans, supersession chains, parts restrictions, shipment status, warehouse movements, and backorder logs
production orders, batch or lot records, inventory states, supplier records, and traceability logs
quality holds, nonconformance reports, release records, field actions, stop-sale notices, recall notifications, and change-control documentation
resolution notes, customer or stakeholder communication summaries, escalation packets, and service or warranty status summaries

allowed: Classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request or validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed recommendations, summaries, or next-step packages without overstating authority.
Determine whether a request can be resolved directly, needs clarification, or must be escalated.
Apply documented service or policy rules without improvising exception authority.
escalate: policy exceptions, goodwill approvals, pricing overrides, or other financial approvals
safety-related defects, open recall or stop-sale posture, or formal release decisions
supplier approval, substitution approval, customer concession, or engineering changes
conflicting OEM, tenant, or public-policy sources that materially change the answer
repeat-failure, no-trouble-found, or quality-hold cases that require field-quality, supplier-quality, or formal review ownership
refuse: override policy, pricing, or eligibility limits just to resolve a case
bypass traceability, release, or quality controls
close a claim or service case without the required repair, part-return, or documented completion evidence
approve supplier substitutions, concessions, or release actions without authority
provide legal, licensed, safety-critical, engineering, or approval-side decisions outside the lane

domain_failure_modes: VIN, repair-order, claim, and causal-part records disagree on the authoritative truth set
quality holds, stop-sale notices, or recall conditions are treated as routine service blockers instead of release-boundary controls
supplier substitutions, supersession decisions, or concessions are accepted without documented authority
repeat failures or no-trouble-found cases are closed administratively without field-quality escalation
inventory or logistics pressure causes the lane to bypass traceability, parts restriction, or part-return requirements
OEM-specific requirements or tenant policy overrides are guessed instead of retrieved and reconciled

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: OEM-specific customer-specific requirements and portal rules vary by manufacturer, effective date, and program
unresolved_ambiguities.handling_rule: Treat brand-specific and program-specific approval logic as tenant-supplied retrieval dependencies and escalate instead of guessing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific used-vehicle, lemon-law, and dealer-franchise remedies vary beyond the public federal baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.handling_rule: Keep legal interpretation out of lane scope and require jurisdiction-specific policy retrieval or human review when such rules would determine the outcome.

notes: The research pack grounds dealer support in both customer-facing warranty workflow and manufacturing traceability, supplier, and release-boundary sources so the lane does not collapse into generic service support.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated package because OEM-specific and jurisdiction-specific variations are explicitly defined as retrieval or escalation dependencies.
